### 1. Calculating the Area and Diagonal of the Golden Rectangle

Given:
- The width of the rectangle = 2 meters
- The golden ratio (φ) = (1 + √5) / 2 ≈ 1.618

The length (L) of the rectangle is calculated using the golden ratio:
$$
L = \text{width} \times \phi = 2 \times \left( \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} \right) = 1 + \sqrt{5}
$$

#### Area of the Rectangle
$$
\text{Area} = \text{width} \times \text{length} = 2 \times (1 + \sqrt{5}) = 2 + 2\sqrt{5} \, \text{square meters}
$$

#### Length of the Diagonal
Using the Pythagorean theorem:
$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{width}^2 + \text{length}^2} = \sqrt{2^2 + (1 + \sqrt{5})^2}
$$
$$
= \sqrt{4 + (1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5)} = \sqrt{4 + 6 + 2\sqrt{5}} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}} \, \text{meters}
$$

---

### 2. Calculating the Radius and Area Outside the Circle

#### Radius of the Inscribed Circle
The radius (r) of the circle inscribed within a rectangle is half of the shorter side of the rectangle. Since the width is the shorter side:
$$
r = \frac{\text{width}}{2} = \frac{2}{2} = 1 \, \text{meter}
$$

#### Area of the Circle
$$
\text{Area}_{\text{circle}} = \pi r^2 = \pi (1)^2 = \pi \, \text{square meters}
$$

#### Area Inside the Rectangle but Outside the Circle
$$
\text{Area}_{\text{outside}} = \text{Area}_{\text{rectangle}} - \text{Area}_{\text{circle}} = (2 + 2\sqrt{5}) - \pi \, \text{square meters}
$$
